human_verification.notes: Verified healthcare-specific documentation integrity scope, authoritative source classes, PHI and minimum-necessary handling, amendment and ROI boundaries, clinical-versus-administrative refusal rules, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 13 scenarios pass with explicit healthcare documentation, ROI, amendment, deficiency, PHI, source-conflict, and adjacent-authority behavior.
Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and both healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.
Research summary covers all required healthcare source classes and domain scenario families with no blocking unresolved ambiguity.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack names healthcare documentation artifacts including encounter notes, consents, ROI requests, amendments, attestations, signatures, deficiency queues, disclosure logs, legal health record definitions, and designated record set definitions.

Boundary handling is explicit for clinical authorship, note rewriting, medical necessity, coding, billing, payer interpretation, privacy-law decisioning, legal-hold decisioning, and final release or amendment authority.

Required tenant facts include jurisdiction, care setting, source-of-truth hierarchy, record locations, patient or member status, service dates, payer or plan, authorization, referral, consent, access, and minimum-necessary posture.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; state-law, tenant policy, source-precedence, payer, and sensitive-record variance is converted into required retrieval or escalation behavior.
Package is ready for validated spec-pack use and future retained-implementation promotion if repeated runtime demand justifies it.
